rulings.Case Title: SHELDON FONG VS. EAST WEST BANK
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motionfora\Vard Of Attorneys Fees And Statutory Costs Pursuant To Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday September 14, 2015, line 1. Defendant's Motion for Attorney's Fees and Costs is continued to October 8, 2015 to be heard with other pending motion. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: SHELDON FONG VS. EAST WEST BANK
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Or Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday September 14, 2015, line 1. Plaintiff's Motion to Strike or Tax Costs is continued to October 8, 2015. Opposing party is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6B and provide courtesy copy of opposition to department 501 no later than September 25, 2015 with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: EAGLE VISTA EQUITIES, LLC VS. JAVIER ANTUNEZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday September 14, 2015, line 6. Defendant's Demurrer to Complaint is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently alleges a cause of action for unlawful detainer under CCP 1161a. Defendant's claims of tenancy and entitlement to SFRO protections are questions of fact not appropriately raised by demurrer. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: LUCY P DESCHAUMES ET AL VS. CARMELITA ROTH ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday September 14, 2015, line 7. Defendant's Motion to Quash is DENIED. Order authorizing service by Post and Mail on file. Proof of Service by a registered process server on file. Defendant presents no admissible competent evidence to rebut the presumption of proper service. Additionally, motion is not properly set under CCP 1167.4. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHER L. SAMUELSON VS. LEO PAVEL SAMUELSON
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday September 14, 2015, line 8. Defendant's Demurer to Complaint is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Applicability of CC 1946.1 is based on improperly raised issues of fact, i.e. complaint does not allege that defendant is a tenant whose tenancy is being terminated. Complaint is not uncertain as the premises are described with reasonable certainty. = (501/REQ)
